task_completion.description: Verify the bedside Multiparameter Monitor and Vital Signs application are active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE interface shows the 'Multiparameter Monitor' device running/connected, and the 'Vital Signs' clinical application is open and displaying patient data.

task_completion.description: Verify the portable Pulse Oximeter is activated alongside the bedside monitor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE interface shows the 'Pulse Oximeter' device has been started and is running at the same time as the Multiparameter Monitor.

task_completion.description: Verify the overlap screenshot was captured and saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image file named 'handoff_overlap.png' is visible on the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop) or is shown open in an image viewer, depicting both the Multiparameter Monitor and Pulse Oximeter active simultaneously.

task_completion.description: Verify the bedside Multiparameter Monitor is disconnected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Multiparameter Monitor' in the OpenICE interface is shown as stopped, disconnected, or no longer sending data.

task_completion.description: Verify the Vital Signs application remains active and receives data after the bedside monitor is disconnected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Vital Signs' application is still open and actively displaying data (now sourced from the Pulse Oximeter) despite the Multiparameter Monitor being disconnected.

task_completion.description: Verify the transport log file is created with the correct contents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text file named 'transport_log.txt' is visible on the Desktop or open in a text editor, containing the exact phrases 'Overlap achieved' and 'Bedside disconnected'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the OpenICE GUI to manage devices.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenICE graphical user interface being clicked or manipulated, rather than just terminal commands mocking the device states.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot is a genuine capture of the desktop state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'handoff_overlap.png' file should look like a real screenshot of the OpenICE environment, not a downloaded stock image, a blank file, or a manually drawn image.

integrity.description: Verify the log file was created legitimately.
integrity.visual_evidence: The log file should be created via a text editor (like gedit or nano) or standard terminal echo commands, rather than being hallucinated or copied from a pre-existing unrelated file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the OpenICE simulator. The bedside device is a 'Multiparameter Monitor', the transport device is a 'Pulse Oximeter', and the clinical app is 'Vital Signs'. The overlap screenshot must be saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/handoff_overlap.png' and show both devices active simultaneously. The log file must be at '/home/ga/Desktop/transport_log.txt' containing 'Overlap achieved' and 'Bedside disconnected'. The pass threshold is 60/100.
